is_p_falciparum.evidence: Study of P. falciparum isolates from 200 children treated with AL in Zanzibar.

reports_target_markers.evidence: Reports pfmdr1 N86Y (position 86) frequencies in Table 1 (86N: 23.4% at day 0, 47.4% in reinfections).

sub_country_location.evidence: All samples collected in Zanzibar; frequencies pooled across the whole trial with no sub-national admin unit, town, clinic, or site-level breakdown reported.

temporal_resolution_within_3y.evidence: Trial conducted between October 2002 and February 2003, well within 3 years.

african_field_samples.evidence: Field/clinical isolates from children with uncomplicated malaria in Zanzibar, Tanzania.

is_primary_study.evidence: Primary molecular analysis of samples from a clinical trial.

duplicate_risk.evidence: Samples are from a previously published clinical trial (Martensson et al. 2005; Sisowath et al. 2005) and re-examine the same trial samples: 'we have re-examined samples from the same trial'.

supplement.evidence: No supplementary materials referenced for finer spatial data; the location is simply Zanzibar with no sub-national breakdown available.

assessment.summary: Molecular study of pfmdr1 (including position 86) and other SNPs in 200 children treated with artemether-lumefantrine in Zanzibar (Oct 2002–Feb 2003). Reports extractable frequencies for pfmdr1 86N with numerators/denominators, but data are pooled at the Zanzibar level with no sub-national resolution. Also a re-analysis of previously published trial samples, indicating high duplicate risk.
exclusion_reasons: No sub-country spatial resolution: frequencies are pooled for Zanzibar with no sub-national site/clinic breakdown
High duplicate risk: re-examination of samples from a previously published clinical trial (Martensson et al. 2005; Sisowath et al. 2005)
